body:
status=open
reason=Execution environment hit ENOSPC (no space left on device) partway through investigation. The Bash tool cannot even mkdir its per-session working directory (/tmp/claude-501/...), so no shell commands (go test, lefthook, git) can be run in this worktree at all. This is a host-level disk-space exhaustion issue, not a defect in the bead's target code, and is retryable once disk space is reclaimed on the execution host. Investigation completed before the environment failed: - Read cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go: runDoctorUnjam/unjamExecuteBeadWorktrees currently only handles stale execute-bead worktree pruning; it never touches bead-tracker phantom-dependency repair. - Read cli/internal/bead/doctor.go: BeadDoctorFix (dangling_dep + parent_ancestor_in_deps repair, archive-aware via loadArchiveBeadIDs, backup under .ddx/backups/, idempotent) already implements the exact remediation the bead asks to wire into unjam. - Read cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go: shows the existing `ddx bead doctor --fix` wiring calling bead.BeadDoctorFix(path, nil) where path = ddxroot.JoinProject(workspace, "beads.jsonl"). - Read cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go: existing worktree-prune tests use setupDoctorUnjamRepo/executeWithStdoutCapture/decodeDoctorUnjamReport helpers that the new phantom-dep tests (AC1-3) should extend (report struct needs new fields for bead-doctor repair findings/backup path/idempotency). Remaining work once the environment is unblocked: 1. Add bead-doctor findings/actions fields to doctorUnjamReport in cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go (e.g. BeadDoctorFindings, BeadDoctorBackupPath) and call bead.BeadDoctorFix against the workspace's beads.jsonl inside unjamExecuteBeadWorktrees (or a sibling step called from runDoctorUnjam), converting bead.DoctorReport findings into doctorUnjamAction entries. Only surface dangling_dep-driven repairs are in scope per the bead's NON-SCOPE note, but BeadDoctorFix also fixes oversized_field/parent_ancestor findings — that is acceptable since it's the existing --fix behavior, not new scope. 2. Resolve the bead tracker workspace root consistently with f.beadWorkspaceRoot()/ddxroot.JoinProject so unjam operates on the same beads.jsonl as `ddx bead doctor --fix`. 3. Add TestDoctorUnjam_PrunesPhantomDeps, TestDoctorUnjam_PreservesArchivedDependencyTargets, TestDoctorUnjam_BeadDoctorRepairSummaryAndIdempotency to cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go per AC1-3. 4. Verify cli/internal/bead/doctor_test.go TestBeadDoctor_DetectsAndFixesDanglingDep is untouched/still green (AC4) and run `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'DoctorUnjam' -count=1` plus `lefthook run pre-commit` once tooling is available (AC5-6). No code was modified in this attempt because no test/build/lint command could be executed to validate any change against a nearly-full or full disk.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: Execution environment hit ENOSPC (no space left on device) partway through investigation. The Bash tool cannot even mkdir its per-session working directory (/tmp/claude-501/...), so no shell commands (go test, lefthook, git) can be run in this worktree at all. This is a host-level disk-space exhaustion issue, not a defect in the bead's target code, and is retryable once disk space is reclaimed on the execution host.

Investigation completed before the environment failed:
- Read cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go: runDoctorUnjam/unjamExecuteBeadWorktrees currently only handles stale execute-bead worktree pruning; it never touches bead-tracker phantom-dependency repair.
- Read cli/internal/bead/doctor.go: BeadDoctorFix (dangling_dep + parent_ancestor_in_deps repair, archive-aware via loadArchiveBeadIDs, backup under .ddx/backups/, idempotent) already implements the exact remediation the bead asks to wire into unjam.
- Read cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go: shows the existing `ddx bead doctor --fix` wiring calling bead.BeadDoctorFix(path, nil) where path = ddxroot.JoinProject(workspace, "beads.jsonl").
- Read cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go: existing worktree-prune tests use setupDoctorUnjamRepo/executeWithStdoutCapture/decodeDoctorUnjamReport helpers that the new phantom-dep tests (AC1-3) should extend (report struct needs new fields for bead-doctor repair findings/backup path/idempotency).

Remaining work once the environment is unblocked:
1. Add bead-doctor findings/actions fields to doctorUnjamReport in cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go (e.g. BeadDoctorFindings, BeadDoctorBackupPath) and call bead.BeadDoctorFix against the workspace's beads.jsonl inside unjamExecuteBeadWorktrees (or a sibling step called from runDoctorUnjam), converting bead.DoctorReport findings into doctorUnjamAction entries. Only surface dangling_dep-driven repairs are in scope per the bead's NON-SCOPE note, but BeadDoctorFix also fixes oversized_field/parent_ancestor findings — that is acceptable since it's the existing --fix behavior, not new scope.
2. Resolve the bead tracker workspace root consistently with f.beadWorkspaceRoot()/ddxroot.JoinProject so unjam operates on the same beads.jsonl as `ddx bead doctor --fix`.
3. Add TestDoctorUnjam_PrunesPhantomDeps, TestDoctorUnjam_PreservesArchivedDependencyTargets, TestDoctorUnjam_BeadDoctorRepairSummaryAndIdempotency to cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go per AC1-3.
4. Verify cli/internal/bead/doctor_test.go TestBeadDoctor_DetectsAndFixesDanglingDep is untouched/still green (AC4) and run `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'DoctorUnjam' -count=1` plus `lefthook run pre-commit` once tooling is available (AC5-6).

No code was modified in this attempt because no test/build/lint command could be executed to validate any change against a nearly-full or full disk.
